author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew VII. 21.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of Heaven, but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore said He not, “but he that doeth my will?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because for the time it was a great gainfor them to receive even this first; yea it was very great, considering their weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And moreover He intimated the one also by the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And withal this may be mentioned, that in fact there is no other will of the Son besides that of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here He seems to me to be censuring the Jews chiefly, laying as they did the whole stress upon the doctrines, and taking no care of practice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which Paul also blames them, saying, “Behold thou art called a Jew, and restest in the law, and makest thy boast of God, and knowest His will:”“Who then are these men?” you ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Many of them that believed received gifts such as he that was casting out devils,and was not with Him; such as Judas; for even he too, wicked as he was, had a gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in the Old Testament also this may be found, in that grace hath oftentimes wrought upon unworthy persons, that it might do good to others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, since all men were not meet for all things, but some were of a pure life, not having so great faith, and others just the contrary; by these sayings, while He urges the one to show forth much faith, the others too He was summoning by this His unspeakable gift to become better men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also with great abundance did He bestow that grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “we wrought,” it is said, “many mighty works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But “then will I profess unto them, I knew you not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “now indeed they suppose they are my friends; but then shall they know, that not as to friends did I give to them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why marvel if He hath bestowed gifts on men that have believed on Him, though without life suitable to their faith, when even on those who have fallen from both these, He is unquestionably found working?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so Balaam was an alien both from faith and from a truly good life; nevertheless grace wrought on him for the serviceof other men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Pharaoh too was of the same sort: yet for all that even to him He signified the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Nebuchadnezzar was very full of iniquity; yet to him again He revealed what was to follow after many generations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again to the son of this last, though surpassing his father in iniquity, He signified the things to come, ordering a marvellous and great dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly because then also the beginnings of the gospel were taking place, and it was requisite that the manifestation of its power should be abundant, many even of the unworthy used to receive gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Howbeit, from those miracles no gain accrued to them; rather they are the more punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore unto them did He utter even that fearful saying, “I never knew you:” there being many for whom His hatred begins already even here; whom He turns away from, even before the judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us fear therefore, beloved; and let us take great heed to our life, neither let us account ourselves worse off, in that we do not work miracles now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that will never be any advantage to us, as neither any disadvantage in our not working them, if we take heed to all virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because for the miracles we ourselves are debtors, but for our life and our doings we have God our debtor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having now, you see, finished all, having discoursed accurately of all virtue, and pointed out the pretenders to it, of divers kinds, both such as for display fast and make prayers, and such as come in the sheep’s hide; and them too that spoil it, whom He also called swine and dogs: He proceeds to signify how great is the profit of virtue even here, and how great the mischief of wickedness, by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Whosoever therefore heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, shall be likened unto a wise man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus: What they shall suffer who do not (although they work miracles), ye have heard; but ye should know also what such as obey all these sayings shall enjoy; not in the world to come only, but even here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For whosoever,” saith He, “heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, shall be likened to a wise man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He varies His discourse; at one time saying, “Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord,” and revealing Himself; at another time, “He that doeth the will of my Father;” and again, bringing in Himself as judge, “For many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name, and I will say, I know you not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here again He indicates Himself to have the power over all, this being why He said, “Whosoever heareth these sayings of mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus whereas all His discourse had been touching the future; of a kingdom, and an unspeakable reward and consolation, and the like; His will is, out of things here also to give them their fruits, and to signify how great is the strength of virtue even in the present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is this her strength?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To live in safety, to be easily subdued by no terror, to stand superior to all that despitefully use us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To this what can be equal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this, not even he that wears the diadem can provide for himself, but that man who follows after virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he alone is possessed of it in full abundance: in the ebb and flowof the things present he enjoys a great calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The truly marvellous thing being this, that not in fair weather, but when the storm is vehement, and the turmoil great, and the temptations continual, he cannot be shaken ever so little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the rain descended,” saith He, “the floods came, the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon the rock.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By “rain” here, and “floods,” and “winds,” He is expressing metaphorically the calamities and afflictions that befall men; such as false accusations, plots, bereavements, deaths, loss of friends, vexations from strangers, all the ills in our life that any one could mention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But to none of these,” saith He, “doth such a soul give way; and the cause is, it is founded on the rock.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He calls the stedfastness of His doctrine a rock; because in truth His commands are stronger than any rock, setting one above all the waves of human affairs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who keeps these things strictly, will not have the advantage of men only when they are vexing him, but even of the very devils plotting against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that it is not vain boasting so to speak, Job is our witness, who received all the assaults of the devil, and stood unmoveable; and the apostles too are our witnesses, for that when the waves of the whole world were beating against them, when both nations and princes, both their own people and strangers, both the evil spirits, and the devil, and every engine was set in motion, they stood firmer than a rock, and dispersed it all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now, what can be happier than this kind of life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this, not wealth, not strength of body, not glory, not power, nor ought else will be able to secure, but only the possession of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is not, nay there is not another life we may find free from all evils, but this alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ye are witnesses, who know the plots in king’s courts, the turmoils and the troubles in the houses of the rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there was not among the apostles any such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did no such thing befall them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they suffer no evil at any man’s hand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, the marvel is this above all things, that they were indeed the object of many plots, and many storms burst upon them, but their soul was not overset by them, nor thrown into despair, but with naked bodies they wrestled, prevailed, and triumphed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou then likewise, if thou be willing to perform these things exactly, shall laugh all ills to scorn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for if thou be but strengthened with such philosophy as is in these admonitions, nothing shall be able to hurt thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since in what is he to harm thee, who is minded to lay plots?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will he take away thy money?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well, but before their threatening thou wast commanded to despise it, and to abstain from it so exceedingly, as not so much as even to ask any such thing of thy Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But doth he cast thee into prison?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, before thy prison, thou wast enjoined so to live, as to be crucified even to all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But doth he speak evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, from this pain also Christ hath delivered thee, by promising thee without toil a great reward for the endurance of evil, and making thee so clear from the anger and vexation hence arising, as even to command thee to pray for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But doth he banish thee and involve thee in innumerable ills?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well, he is making the crown more glorious for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But doth he destroy and murder thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even hereby he profits thee very greatly, procuring for thee the rewards of the martyrs, and conducting thee more quickly into the untroubled haven, and affording thee matter for a more abundant recompence, and contriving for thee to make a gain of the universal penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which thing indeed is most marvellous of all, that the plotters, so far from injuring at all, do rather make the objects of their despite more approved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To this what can be comparable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, to the choice of such a mode of life as this, and no other, is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus whereas He had called the way strait and narrow; to soothe our labors on this side also, He signifies the security thereof to be great, and great the pleasure; even as of the opposite course great is the unsoundness, and the detriment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as virtue even from things here was signified by Him to have her rewards, so vice also her penalties.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what I am ever saying, that I will say now also: that in both ways He is everywhere bringing about the salvation of His hearers on the one hand by zeal for virtue, on the other by hatred of vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, because there would be some to admire what He said, while they yield no proof of it by their works, He by anticipation awakens their fears, saying, Though the things spoken be good, hearing is not sufficient for security, but there is need also of obedience in actions, and the whole lies chiefly in this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here He ends His discourse, leaving the fear at its height in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as with regard to virtue, not only from the things to come did He urge them (speaking of a kingdom, and of Heaven, and an unspeakable reward, and comfort, and the unnumbered good things): but also from the things present, indicating the firm and immoveable quality of the Rock; so also with respect to wickedness, not from the expected things only doth He excite their fears (as from the tree that is cut down, and the unquenchable fire, and the not entering into the kingdom, and from His saying, “I know you not”): but also from the things present, the downfall, I mean, in what is said of the house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He made His argument more expressive, by trying its forcein a parable; for it was not the same thing to say, “The virtuous man shall be impregnable, but the wicked easily subdued,” as to suppose a rock, and a house, and rivers, and rain, and wind, and the like.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And every one,” saith He, “that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened to a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well did He call this man “foolish”: for what can be more senseless than one building a house on the sand, and while he submits to the labor, depriving himself of the fruit and refreshment, and instead thereof undergoing punishment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they too, who follow after wickedness, do labor, is surely manifest to every one: since both the extortioner, and the adulterer, and the false accuser, toil and weary themselves much to bring their wickedness to effect; but so far from reaping any profit from these their labors, they rather undergo great loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Paul too intimated this when he said, “He that soweth to his flesh, shall of his flesh reap corruption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To this man are they like also, who build on the sand; as those that are given up to fornication, to wantonness, to drunkenness, to anger, to all the other things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such an one was Ahab, but not such Elijah (since when we have put virtue and vice along side of one another, we shall know more accurately the difference): for the one had built upon the rock, the other on the sand; wherefore though he were a king, he feared and trembled at the prophet, at him that had only his sheepskin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such were the Jews but not the apostles; and so though they were few and in bonds, they exhibited the steadfastness of the rock; but those, many as they were, and in armor, the weakness of the sand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so they said, “What shall we do to these men?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou those in perplexity, not who are in the hands of others, and bound, but who are active in holding down and binding?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what can be more strange than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou hold of the other, and art yet in utter perplexity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes, and very naturally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For inasmuch as they had built all on the sand, therefore also were they weaker than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause also they said again, “What do ye, seeking to bring this man’s blood upon us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou scourge, and art thou in fear? entreatest thou despitefully, and art in dismay?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou judge, and yet tremble?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So feeble is wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the Apostles not so, but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou a noble spirit? seest thou a rock laughing waves to scorn? seest thou a house unshaken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is yet more marvellous; so far from turning cowards themselves at the plots formed against them, they even took more courage, and cast the others into greater anxiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so he that smites adamant, is himself the one smitten; and he that kicks against the pricks, is himself the one pricked, the one on whom the severe wounds fall: and he who is forming plots against the virtuous, is himself the one in jeopardy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wickedness becomes so much the weaker, the more it sets itself in array against virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as he who wraps up fire in a garment, extinguishes not the flame, but consumes the garment; so he that is doing despite to virtuous men, and oppressing them, and binding them, makes them more glorious, but destroys himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more ills thou sufferest, living righteously, the stronger art thou become; since the more we honor self-restraint, the less we need anything; and the less we need anything, the stronger we grow, and the more above all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such a one was John; wherefore him no man pained, but he caused pain to Herod; so he that had nothing prevailed against him that ruled; and he that wore a diadem, and purple, and endless pomp, trembles, and is in fear of him that is stripped of all, and not even when beheaded could he without fear see his head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that even after his death he had the terror of him in full strength, hear what He saith, “This is John, whom I slew.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now the expression, “I slew,” is that of one not exulting, but soothing his own terror, and persuading his troubled soul to call to mind, that he himself slew him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great is the force of virtue, that even after death it is more powerful than the living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this same cause again, when he was living, they that possessed much wealth came unto him, and said, “What shall we do?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is so much yours, and are ye minded to learn the way of your prosperity from him that hath nothing? the rich from the poor? the soldiers from him that hath not even a house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such an one was Elias too: wherefore also with the same freedom did he discourse to the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as the former said, “Ye generation of vipers;”so this latter, “How long will ye halt upon both your hips?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the one said, “Hast thou killed, and inherited?”the other, “It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother Philip’s wife.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the rock?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the sand; how easily it sinks down, how it yields to calamities? how it is overthrown, though it have the support of royalty, of number, of nobility?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For them that pursue it, it makes more senseless than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it doth not merely fall, but with great calamity: for “great indeed,” He saith, “was the fall of it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The risk not being of trifles, but of the soul, of the loss of Heaven, and those immortal blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather even before that loss, no life so wretched as he must live that follows after this; dwelling with continual despondencies, alarms, cares, anxieties; which a certain wise man also was intimating when he said, “The wicked fleeth, when no man is pursuing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such men tremble at their shadows, suspect their friends, their enemies, their servants, such as know them, such as know them not; and before their punishment, suffer extreme punishment here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to declare all this, Christ said, “And great was the fall of it;” shutting up these good commandments with that suitable ending, and persuading even by the things present the most unbelieving to flee from vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although the argument from what is to come be vaster, yet is this of more power to restrain the grosser sort, and to withdraw them from wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also he ended with it, that the profit thereof might make its abode in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Conscious therefore of all these things, both the present, and the future, let us flee from vice, let us emulate virtue, that we may not labor fruitlessly and at random, but may both enjoy the security here, and partake of the glory there: unto which God grant we may all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory and the might forever and ever.
